projectDescription: A Windows security research proof-of-concept demonstrating a vulnerability chain that abuses Windows Defender's privileged update mechanism to extract the SAM database and escalate privileges via NTLM hash decryption.
keyFeatures: Windows Defender RPC abuse via ServerMpUpdateEngineSignature to trigger privileged file operations
Volume Shadow Copy (VSS) creation timing exploit using EICAR test file and oplock synchronization
Windows Defender freeze via Cloud Files API (CldApi) callback and batch oplock on update file
NT object manager symbolic link injection to redirect WD's privileged file read to the SAM hive
Offline SAM hive parsing with LSA boot key extraction and AES/DES NTLM hash decryption
Privilege escalation by temporarily changing user passwords and launching processes as admin/SYSTEM
publicApiSurface: CallWD() — invokes ServerMpUpdateEngineSignature via Windows Defender ALPC/RPC
TriggerWDForVS() — orchestrates VSS creation by triggering WD with EICAR file
FreezeVSS() — freezes WD mid-scan using Cloud Files sync root and batch oplock
ShadowCopyFinderThread() — detects newly created VSS volume in NT object directory
DoSpawnShellAsAllUsers() — reads SAM hive, decrypts hashes, spawns shells as each local user
GetLSASecretKey() — extracts the Windows boot key from LSA registry subkeys
UnprotectNTHash() — decrypts SAM NTLM password hashes using AES+DES key derivation
ChangeUserPassword() — changes a local user's password via SamiChangePasswordUser RPC
